title: Unit 7: Singly Linked List
description: Learn how to build dynamic lists of nodes connected by pointers and solve core interview tasks.

topics.title: 7.1 Singly Linked List Basic Operations
topics.description: Creating a node class, traversal, insertion, and deletion. Nodes store data and references to next elements.

complexity.time: O(1) head operations, O(n) traversal/arbitrary position

ispo.desc: Accepts integer value to append as a new list node.
Defines class representation Node containing value and pointer next in memory.
Stores head reference variable pointing to the first node of list.
Creates new Node object in memory to hold the value.
Declares current node reference to traverse the list.
Hooks newNode.next pointer to existing head, then updates head to point to newNode.
Iterates through the linked list, following the next reference until null is reached.
Prints the value of each node sequentially to standard output.
Prints the null terminator at the end of traversal.

quiz.q: What is the time complexity of deleting a node directly after a given node in a Singly Linked List?

topics.title: 7.2 Linked List Cycle
topics.description: Determine if a linked list contains a cycle using Floyd's Cycle Finding Algorithm (two-pointer technique with fast and slow pointers).

ispo.desc: Accepts head reference node.
Allocates slow and fast pointer references in storage memory.
Steps slow pointer by 1 node and fast pointer by 2 nodes. Checks if they intersect.
Returns false if list is empty.
Returns true when slow and fast meet, indicating a cycle loop exists.
Returns false if fast pointer reaches end of list.

quiz.q: Why does Floyd's Cycle Finding Algorithm use slow and fast pointers shifting at different speeds?
o: If a cycle exists, the fast pointer will eventually catch up and meet the slow pointer within the loop

topics.title: 7.3 Remove Linked List Elements
topics.description: Remove all elements from a linked list that have a specific value. Handles head deletions using a dummy sentinel node.

ispo.desc: Takes list head and value to delete.
Creates dummy sentinel node and current pointer to walk the list.
Iterates. If the next node matches value, redirects next pointer to skip it; otherwise, moves current forward.
Returns dummy.next (the new head node pointer).

quiz.q: What is the primary benefit of utilizing a 'dummy' sentinel node in linked list deletions?
o: Prevents code duplication and simplifies handling deletion of the head node

topics.title: 7.4 Reverse Linked List
topics.description: Reverse a singly linked list in place by changing the direction of node pointers.

ispo.desc: Receives head pointer of list.
Creates prev and current reference variables in storage memory to execute pointer redirection.
Allocates temporary pointer nextTemp to record the next node before pointer redirection.
Saves next, flips current.next to point backward to prev, and slides prev and current forward.
Returns prev, which is the head pointer of the inverted list.

quiz.q: What is the value of 'current.next' after a single pointer inversion step on current node?
o: The next node in original order

topics.title: 7.5 Palindrome Linked List
topics.description: Determine if a singly linked list is a palindrome. Walk to middle (using fast/slow), reverse the second half, and compare values.
topics.cheatSheet:
1. Find middle with slow/fast pointers.
2. Reverse second half starting from middle.next.
3. Compare first half values with reversed second half.

ispo.desc: Accepts head node parameter.
Declares slow and fast tracking pointers.
Allocates p1, p2, and secondHead references to compare halves.
Declares boolean indicator 'palindrome'.
Walks slow pointer to middle of list.
Invokes list reversal helper starting from middle node's next slot.
Iterates and compares data values of first and second half nodes.
Returns true/false palindrome outcome.

quiz.q: What is the optimal space complexity for checking if a linked list is a palindrome?

topics.title: 7.6 Middle of the Linked List
topics.description: Find the middle node of a singly linked list. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node. Uses slow and fast pointers.

ispo.desc: Takes list head node.
Creates slow and fast reference indicators.
Moves slow pointer by 1 step and fast pointer by 2 steps. When fast reaches end, slow is at the middle.
Returns slow node pointer (middle).

quiz.q: For list 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 6 -> null, which node does getMiddle return?

topics.title: 7.7 Convert Binary number in LL to Integer
topics.description: Given a singly linked list representing a binary number, convert it to its decimal integer value. Traverse left-to-right shifting values.

ispo.desc: Accepts head node representing start of binary number.
Allocates decimalValue accumulator and current traversal pointer.
Iterates. Shifts existing value left (multiplies by 2) and adds current node's bit value (0 or 1).
Returns final converted integer value.

quiz.q: What decimal value is returned for binary list 1 -> 0 -> 1 -> null?
